Use Euler's identity involving 2i at some point

One possible solution starts by square rooting 24 times. Use base change formula
24 = 4! and 4 is a power of 2. Taking a square root is the same as raising to the power of 1/2
Sine turns numbers that aren't changed by square root into numbers that are changed by square root (and log-able)
Make -√0.5+(√0.5)i from √0.5+(√0.5)i and √2. Notice this has arg = 3π/4. Once we obtain 3, we're only off by a power of 2
Make -√0.5-(√0.5)i and √0.5-(√0.5)i. From these, it is possible to obtain 3, which is only off by a power of 2
Make -√0.5+(√0.5)i out of two numbers and √0.5+(√0.5)i out of one number. Now, base change formula
Generalize exploit: 24 from log base c^(1/32) of c^(3/4)
There's a solution where, at some point, you divide two expressions to obtain 2π
